title: Add Stable Diffusion negative prompt presets

requirements.text: Add a negative prompt preset selector to the ImageGenerationForm component with at least 5 preset options (e.g., 'Low Quality', 'Anatomical Issues', 'Artifacts', 'Default Safe', 'None') that users can choose from a dropdown

quotes: add Stable Diffusion negative prompt presets

acceptanceCriteria: A dropdown/select input is added to the form for selecting negative prompt presets
At least 5 negative prompt preset options are available for selection
The selected preset is stored in the GenerationParams state alongside other parameters
The form validates that a negative prompt preset is selected before submission

requirements.text: Define negative prompt preset text values for each preset option that contain appropriate Stable Diffusion negative prompt keywords (e.g., 'Low Quality' preset includes keywords like 'low quality, blurry, pixelated, distorted'; 'Anatomical Issues' includes 'bad anatomy, deformed, mutated'; etc.)

quotes: add Stable Diffusion negative prompt presets

acceptanceCriteria: Each preset option maps to a specific negative prompt text value with relevant keywords
Negative prompt texts are comprehensive and appropriate for Stable Diffusion
Presets cover common quality issues, anatomical problems, and artifact prevention

requirements.text: Update the PoseCriteria type in backend/main.mo to include a negativePrompt field of type Text to store the selected negative prompt preset text
acceptanceCriteria: PoseCriteria type includes a new negativePrompt: Text field
The sendQueries function accepts and stores the negativePrompt value with other criteria
Backend compiles without errors after adding the new field

requirements.text: Update the prompt generation logic in App.tsx to append the selected negative prompt preset text to the generated comprehensive prompt, clearly separating it from the positive prompt (e.g., with a 'Negative prompt:' prefix or similar convention)
acceptanceCriteria: Generated prompt includes both positive and negative prompt sections
Negative prompt is clearly labeled and separated from the positive prompt
The full prompt with negative prompt is displayed to the user after form submission

requirements.text: Add a negative prompt preset badge to the ImageDisplay component that shows the selected preset name alongside the existing parameter badges (body type, height, weight, age, ethnicity, art style)
acceptanceCriteria: A badge displaying the negative prompt preset name appears in the parameter badges grid
The badge follows the same styling pattern as existing parameter badges
The badge is visible after form submission in the ImageDisplay component

requirements.text: Update the useSendQueries mutation hook to include the negativePrompt parameter when calling the backend sendQueries method
acceptanceCriteria: The mutation hook passes the negativePrompt value to the backend
The backend successfully receives and stores the negative prompt with other criteria
No TypeScript or runtime errors occur when submitting the form with negative prompt

requirements.text: Create a migration.mo file to handle upgrading existing PoseCriteria records to include the new negativePrompt field with a default empty string value for backward compatibility
acceptanceCriteria: Migration file transforms old PoseCriteria records to include negativePrompt field
Existing records are preserved with appropriate default values
The upgrade process completes without data loss

constraints: Follow the existing form input pattern used in ImageGenerationForm.tsx for consistency
Use the same styling and layout conventions as existing parameter inputs
Maintain the single-actor architecture in the backend
Ensure backward compatibility with existing stored PoseCriteria records
nonGoals: Implementing actual Stable Diffusion image generation (only prompt generation is in scope)
Adding custom negative prompt text input (presets only)
Changing existing parameter inputs or their behavior
Modifying the NSFW notice or branding elements
